findings.detail: Two parallel panels (same-meaning / unrelated) render cleanly with no overlaps, no truncation, legible subtitles, correct color tiers, and clean convergence onto the shared 0-1 score node. Side-by-side contrast reads as intended: small-angle->score~1 vs wide-angle->score~0.

Mermaid cannot draw free-angle vector glyphs, so the angle->score contrast is carried by the parallel-panel structure plus labels ('small angle'/'wide angle') rather than literally drawn arrows at differing angles. The learning takeaway (smaller angle -> higher score) is preserved and stays within node/scope discipline. Literal angled-arrow glyphs would require Excalidraw; not warranted here.
